AccessCentral.title: Ananta Cloud
AccessCentral.description: Ananta offers a single login page for subscribers and admins alike and redirects based on the type of user who is logging in (subscriber and admin).

AccountCentre/AboutAnantaAccountCentre.title: About Ananta Cloud Support Centre
AccountCentre/AboutAnantaAccountCentre.description: Ananta Cloud Support Centre is the non-technical (commercial and support) dashboard that you can use to view and manage all the non-technical aspects of an Ananta Cloud account.  You can access the Cloud Support Centre by clicking on any item in Top-Right User Menu on the help/support icon in the header, or click the Header > Click the Wallet Icon.

AccountCentre/InvoicesandCreditNotes.title: Invoices and Credit Notes
AccountCentre/InvoicesandCreditNotes.description: Invoices are generated at the end of each billing cycle for the services consumed during the billing cycle. Invoices can be accessed from the Billing > Invoices & Credit Notes section on the main navigation panel.

AccountCentre/Organisation-AccountProfil.title: Organisation/Account Profile
AccountCentre/Organisation-AccountProfil.description: Using the Profile section, you can manage your organisation account profile on Ananta Cloud. The profile management section can be accessed from Account Centre from the top helper bar by clicking on the organisation. Account profiles include the following:

AccountCentre/Payments.title: Payments
AccountCentre/Payments.description: Ananta Cloud supports three ways of making payments or adding money to the wallet:

AccountCentre/StandardAgreements.title: Standard Agreements
AccountCentre/StandardAgreements.description: Standard agreements and documents are available on the Ananta Cloud Console Account Centre. Whenever any of these documents is amended or updated, you will be prompted to accept the modified documents on your next login.

AccountCentre/SubscriptionsandServices.title: Subscriptions and Services
AccountCentre/SubscriptionsandServices.description: A subscription is a record that gets created whenever a new resource is purchased or a service is activated. In other words, a subscription is a financial ‘agreement’ that links the item that has been purchased, its value, and its renewal frequency.

AccountCentre/SupportandTickets.title: Support and Tickets
AccountCentre/SupportandTickets.description: Ananta Cloud provides SLA-driven support to all subscriber customers in accordance with the terms and conditions outlined in the Service Level Agreement. The SLA can be accessed from the top helper bar by clicking on the user icon.

AccountCentre/TeamandChildUserManagement.title: Team and Child User Management
AccountCentre/TeamandChildUserManagement.description: Team members or child users can be onboarded using the TEAM section from the account centre. Ananta Cloud allows you to add billing, technical, or other admin users who can log in to your account and perform operations.

AccountCentre/UnbilledCharges.title: Unbilled Charges
AccountCentre/UnbilledCharges.description: All subscriptions incur prorated charges during an account’s billing cycle. These incurred charges are also known as unbilled charges and can be accessed from the Billing > Unbilled Charges section on the main navigation panel.

AccountCentre/WalletandTransactions.title: Wallet and Transactions
AccountCentre/WalletandTransactions.description: For all practical purposes, the service balance acts as your wallet balance or purchase capacity at any given point in time. The service balance is an aggregation of your credit limit, total payments made and the total charges incurred on your account. Your service balance is always displayed on the top bar and gets updated in real-time when a transaction or a charge is recorded.

CloudExperienceManagement/CloudConsoleOv.title: Cloud Console Overview
CloudExperienceManagement/CloudConsoleOv.description: Ananta Cloud Console is the white-label CMP (cloud management platform) that subscribers and end-customers will use to manage their cloud as delivered by their service provider. Primarily, cloud management on Ananta Cloud Console has two main 'journey maps' that all end-customers follow as part of their life cycle on Ananta. These are:

CloudExperienceManagement/CloudOperation.title: Cloud/Operations Management
CloudExperienceManagement/CloudOperation.description: Setting up and managing cloud resources on Ananta Cloud Console follows a highly intuitive approach that is built on UX principles of modern-day software. The Cloud Console user journeys are simple, visual and information-rich which makes managing cloud resources extremely delightful.

Compute/Autoscale/AboutAutoscale.title: About Autoscale
Compute/Autoscale/AboutAutoscale.description: The Autoscale feature allows a system to adjust its computing resources based on the changing workload automatically. In virtual machines or instances, autoscaling helps ensure that an application or service has the right amount of resources to handle varying levels of demand efficiently. The key concept behind autoscaling is dynamic resource allocation. When demand for a particular application or service increases, autoscaling automatically adds more computing resources (such as instances or virtual machines) to handle the load. Conversely, during periods of lower demand, autoscaling reduces the number of resources to optimize cost and efficiency.

Compute/Autoscale/CreatingAutoscaleGroup.title: Creating Autoscale Groups
Compute/Autoscale/CreatingAutoscaleGroup.description: 1. Go to Compute > Autoscale Groups in the navigation menu.

Compute/Autoscale/RequirementsforAutosca.title: Requirements for Autoscale
Compute/Autoscale/RequirementsforAutosca.description: - Ananta now uses the native Autoscale feature of ACS. This feature is available with ACS version 4.18.0.0 onwards.

Compute/Autoscale/UsingStaticPasswordinA.title: Using Static Password In Autoscale Group’s VMs
Compute/Autoscale/UsingStaticPasswordinA.description: 1. Create a VPC and add a network tier inside the VPC.

Compute/Kubernetes/AboutKubernetesonApic.title: About Kubernetes on Ananta
Compute/Kubernetes/AboutKubernetesonApic.description: Ananta offers Cloud Console users the ability to deploy highly-available Kubernetes clusters for their cloud-native development, deployment and DevOps use cases. The Ananta Kubernetes Service (AK8s) extends basic and advanced cluster management functionalities via the Cloud Console UI and the kubectl utility.

Compute/Kubernetes/AccessingaClusterusin.title: Accessing a Cluster using the Command Line
Compute/Kubernetes/AccessingaClusterusin.description: Kubernetes clusters can be accessed and controlled from the command line using the kubeconfig manifest for the cluster. Each cluster has a unique manifest which is required to identify and target the cluster using the kubectl utility.

Compute/Kubernetes/AccessingtheClusterDa.title: Accessing the Cluster Dashboard
Compute/Kubernetes/AccessingtheClusterDa.description: The Kubernetes Dashboard is a web-based user interface that provides a visual representation of a cluster's resources and enables easier management and monitoring. Here's an overview of the dashboard:

Compute/Kubernetes/ClusterOperations.title: Cluster Operations
Compute/Kubernetes/ClusterOperations.description: Kubernetes clusters created on Ananta Cloud Console allow for a set of management operations from the Cloud Console UI. While these options can be controlled using kubectl, these are provided on the UI for quick and easy access.

Compute/Kubernetes/CreatingaKubernetesCl.title: Creating a Kubernetes Cluster
Compute/Kubernetes/CreatingaKubernetesCl.description: To create a Kubernetes cluster on an advanced VPC AZ, follow these steps:

Compute/Kubernetes/IngressNetworkingonKu.title: Ingress Networking on Kubernetes Clusters
Compute/Kubernetes/IngressNetworkingonKu.description: This Networking section under cluster details helps manage a cluster's firewalls and ingress networking. Port forwarding and load balancing rules can also be managed using kubectl.

Compute/Kubernetes/ScalingKubernetesClus.title: Scaling Kubernetes Clusters
Compute/Kubernetes/ScalingKubernetesClus.description: Ananta Cloud Console allows for manual and automatic cluster scaling. Clusters scaling can be configured from the Nodes section of cluster details.

Compute/Kubernetes/ViewingKubernetesClus.title: Viewing Kubernetes Cluster Details
Compute/Kubernetes/ViewingKubernetesClus.description: Ananta Cloud Console offers a detailed view of Kubernetes clusters on the UI. Ananta also brings the full power and accessibility of cluster management via the kubectl interface.

Compute/LinuxInstances/AboutLinuxInstanc.title: About Linux Instances
Compute/LinuxInstances/AboutLinuxInstanc.description: Instances are computing units that provide you with resources to run your applications/workloads. Linux Instances are virtual machines that run the images of Linux Operating Systems, for example, Ubuntu, CentOS, Rocky, Oracle, Debian, etc.

Compute/LinuxInstances/ConfiguringAlerts.title: Configuring Alerts on Linux Instances
Compute/LinuxInstances/ConfiguringAlerts.description: From Operating Linux Instances, navigate into a Linux Instance and access the Alerts tab to view the configured alerts or configure new ones.

Compute/LinuxInstances/ConnectingtoaLinu.title: Connecting to a Linux Instance
Compute/LinuxInstances/ConnectingtoaLinu.description: You can connect to Linux Instances via SSH sessions using passwords (less secure) or SSH key pairs (most secure)

Compute/LinuxInstances/CreatingLinuxInst.title: Creating Linux Instances
Compute/LinuxInstances/CreatingLinuxInst.description: Before creating a Linux instance, it is important to plan the architecture, networking and access to the Linux Instances.

Compute/LinuxInstances/LinuxInstanceOper.title: Linux Instance Operations
Compute/LinuxInstances/LinuxInstanceOper.description: From Operating Linux Instances, navigate into a Linux Instance and access the Operations tab to see all available Instance operations.

Compute/LinuxInstances/NetworkingManagem.title: Networking Management with Linux Instances
Compute/LinuxInstances/NetworkingManagem.description: To view the Networks associated with Linux Instance, navigate to a Linux Instance and access the Networking tab.

Compute/LinuxInstances/ProtectingLinuxIn.title: Protecting Linux Instances
Compute/LinuxInstances/ProtectingLinuxIn.description: Enabling protection ensures that all instances are protected while all the threats are tracked.

Compute/LinuxInstances/ReconfiguringLinu.title: Reconfiguring Linux Instances
Compute/LinuxInstances/ReconfiguringLinu.description: To access the Reconfigure tab and to view available reconfiguration options, navigate to a Linux Instance from Operating Linux Instances.

Compute/LinuxInstances/ViewingDetailsofL.title: Viewing Details of Linux Instances
Compute/LinuxInstances/ViewingDetailsofL.description: To view the below details, navigate to a Linux Instance from  Operating Linux Instances and access the Overview tab.

Compute/LinuxInstances/ViewingGraphsandU.title: Viewing Graphs and Utilization of Linux Instances

Compute/LinuxInstances/VolumeManagementw.title: Volume Management with Linux Instances
Compute/LinuxInstances/VolumeManagementw.description: From Operating Linux Instances, navigate into a Linux Instance and access the Volumes tab to see the disks attached to this Instance.

Compute/LinuxInstances/WorkingwithLinuxI.title: Working with Linux Instance Snapshots
Compute/LinuxInstances/WorkingwithLinuxI.description: To access the Snapshots tab and to view all the snapshots taken for particular Instance, navigate to a Linux Instance from Operating Linux Instances.

Compute/RHELInstances/AboutRHELInstances.title: About RHEL Instances
Compute/RHELInstances/AboutRHELInstances.description: Instances are computing units that provide you with resources to run your applications/workloads. Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) Instances are virtual machines that run the images of RHEL Operating System.

Compute/RHELInstances/ConfiguringAlertso.title: Configuring Alerts on RHEL Instances
Compute/RHELInstances/ConfiguringAlertso.description: Alerts get triggered whenever a configured condition is met. You can create multiple alerts on an instance. Alerts are sent to recipients that you can define and manage.

Compute/RHELInstances/ConnectingtoaRHELI.title: Connecting to a RHEL Instance
Compute/RHELInstances/ConnectingtoaRHELI.description: You can connect to RHEL Instances via SSH sessions using passwords (less secure) or SSH key pairs (most secure)

Compute/RHELInstances/CreatingRHELInstan.title: Creating RHEL Instances
Compute/RHELInstances/CreatingRHELInstan.description: Before creating a RHEL instance, it is important to plan the architecture, networking and access to the RHEL Instances.

Compute/RHELInstances/NetworkingManageme.title: Networking Management with RHEL Instances
Compute/RHELInstances/NetworkingManageme.description: To view the networks attached to RHEL Instance, navigate to  RHEL Instances, and access the Networking tab

Compute/RHELInstances/ProtectingRHELInst.title: Protecting RHEL Instances
Compute/RHELInstances/ProtectingRHELInst.description: Enabling protection ensures that all instances are protected while all the threats are tracked.

Compute/RHELInstances/ReconfiguringRHELI.title: Reconfiguring RHEL Instances
Compute/RHELInstances/ReconfiguringRHELI.description: To view the available reconfiguration options, navigate to RHEL Instances and access the Reconfigure tab.

Compute/RHELInstances/RHELInstanceOperat.title: RHEL Instance Operations
Compute/RHELInstances/RHELInstanceOperat.description: To view all available Instance operations, from RHEL Instances and access the Operations tab.

Compute/RHELInstances/ViewingDetailsofRH.title: Viewing Details of RHEL Instances
Compute/RHELInstances/ViewingDetailsofRH.description: To view the details below, navigate to RHEL Instance, select a RHEL Instance and access the Overview tab.

Compute/RHELInstances/ViewingGraphsandUt.title: Viewing Graphs and Utilization of RHEL Instances

Compute/RHELInstances/VolumeManagementwi.title: Volume Management with RHEL Instances
Compute/RHELInstances/VolumeManagementwi.description: To view the disk attached to particular instance, navigate to RHEL Instances and access the Volumes tab.

Compute/RHELInstances/WorkingwithRHELIns.title: Working with RHEL Instance Snapshots
Compute/RHELInstances/WorkingwithRHELIns.description: To view all the snapshots for the Instance, navigate to RHEL Instances and access the Snapshots tab.

Compute/WindowsInstances/AboutWindowsIns.title: About Windows Instances
Compute/WindowsInstances/AboutWindowsIns.description: Instances are computing units that provide you with resources to run your applications/workloads. Windows Instances are virtual machines that run the images of Windows Operating Systems.

Compute/WindowsInstances/ConfiguringAler.title: Configuring Alerts on Windows Instances
Compute/WindowsInstances/ConfiguringAler.description: To view the configured alerts or configure new ones, navigate to Operating Windows Instances, select a Windows Instance and access the Alerts tab.

Compute/WindowsInstances/ConnectingtoaWi.title: Connecting to a Windows Instance
Compute/WindowsInstances/ConnectingtoaWi.description: Unlike Linux Instances that use SSH connections, Windows Instances use the remote desktop protocol (RDP).

Compute/WindowsInstances/CreatingWindows.title: Creating Windows Instances
Compute/WindowsInstances/CreatingWindows.description: Before creating a Windows Instance, it is important to plan the architecture, networking and access to the Windows Instances. As a thumb rule:

Compute/WindowsInstances/NetworkingManag.title: Networking Management on Windows Instances
Compute/WindowsInstances/NetworkingManag.description: To view the networks associated with Instance, navigate to Operating Windows Instances, select a Windows Instance and access the Networking tab.

Compute/WindowsInstances/ReconfiguringWi.title: Reconfiguring Windows Instances
Compute/WindowsInstances/ReconfiguringWi.description: From Operating Windows Instances, navigate into a Windows Instance and access the Reconfigure tab to see available reconfiguration options.

Compute/WindowsInstances/ViewingDetailso.title: Viewing Details of Windows Instances
Compute/WindowsInstances/ViewingDetailso.description: To view the details navigate to Operating Windows Instances, select a Windows Instance and access the Overview tab.

Compute/WindowsInstances/ViewingGraphsan.title: Viewing Graphs and Utilisation of Windows Instances

Compute/WindowsInstances/VolumeManagemen.title: Volume Management with Windows Instances
Compute/WindowsInstances/VolumeManagemen.description: To view see the disks attached to Instance, navigate to Operating Windows Instances, select a Windows Instance and access the Volumes tab. Windows Instances on Ananta work with the Block Volumes Service and let you carry out basic disk operations.

Compute/WindowsInstances/WindowsInstance.title: Windows Instance Operations
Compute/WindowsInstances/WindowsInstance.description: To view all the available Instance operations, navigate to Operating Windows Instances, select a Windows Instance and access the Operations.

Compute/WindowsInstances/WorkingwithWind.title: Working with Windows Instance Snapshots
Compute/WindowsInstances/WorkingwithWind.description: To view all the snapshots taken for the instance, navigate to Operating Windows Instances, select a Windows Instance and access the Snapshots tab.

FAQs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
FAQs.description: What happens if I miss a payment due date?

InstanceProtection/AboutInstanceprotecti.title: About Instance protection
InstanceProtection/AboutInstanceprotecti.description: Instance protection safeguards virtual machine instances and workloads by creating backups using Acronis backup solutions. This ensures that end-users can quickly restore the Instances to a previous state in the event of data loss, corruption, or system failure. The service is integrated with Acronis Cyber Protect.

InstanceProtection/ChangingtheProtection.title: Changing the Protection Plan
InstanceProtection/ChangingtheProtection.description: Suppose you want to switch your current plan to a different one. This can be achieved as follows:

InstanceProtection/EnablingInstanceProte.title: Enabling Instance Protection
InstanceProtection/EnablingInstanceProte.description: Instance Protection service can be enabled in two ways.

InstanceProtection/TurningofftheProtecti.title: Turning off the Protection Plan
InstanceProtection/TurningofftheProtecti.description: Protection can be turned off in two ways. The first one is as follows:

InstanceProtection/ViewingProtectedInsta.title: Viewing Protected Instances
InstanceProtection/ViewingProtectedInsta.description: 1. Navigate to Backups and select an Instance Protection.

intro.title: Welcome
intro.description: Welcome to Ananta! As a subscriber, you have access to a powerful platform designed to simplify and enhance the management of your cloud and network services. Ananta offers a range of tools to help you streamline operations, automate tasks, and optimize your digital services.

LoggingIn.title: Logging In
LoggingIn.description: Any user with a valid username/email and password can log in to Ananta and will automatically be directed to their respective control panel based on their user type.

Networking/DNSManagement/AboutDNSManagem.title: About DNS Management
Networking/DNSManagement/AboutDNSManagem.description: Ananta servers can be used as authoritative name server(s) for managing DNS records. If the Service is enabled, Cloud Console users can bring their own domains (BYOD) to use with the workloads created using Ananta-powered clouds.

Networking/DNSManagement/LinkingDomainst.title: Linking Domains to Ananta Cloud Console
Networking/DNSManagement/LinkingDomainst.description: The Ananta-powered cloud will have nameservers if the service provider has enabled this Service. These name servers are simply fully-qualified domain names (FQDN) for the servers that will store all the domain/zone information. Typically, these name servers will be in the following format:

Networking/DNSManagement/ManagingDNSReco.title: Managing DNS Records
Networking/DNSManagement/ManagingDNSReco.description: DNS records and subdomains can be managed on Ananta Cloud Console to be used with Instances, IPv4 Addresses and other workloads created on Ananta-powered clouds. This only requires a domain to be linked with the Ananta nameservers.

Networking/DNSManagement/SwitchingtoAnot.title: Switching to Another DNS Provider
Networking/DNSManagement/SwitchingtoAnot.description: In the event that you don't wish to use the DNS Management Service on your Ananta-powered cloud, you can always switch back to another provider of choice. To do so, simply head to your domain name provider's control panel and update the nameserver details to those of the DNS provider you wish to use.

Networking/FirewallandSecurity/AboutFire.title: About Firewall Instances
Networking/FirewallandSecurity/AboutFire.description: Ananta supports the delivery and management of Virtual Firewall Instances (VFI) in an as-a-service model. VFI is essentially a Linux Instance pre-loaded with a firewall software which makes it work as a a virtual appliance. The Service falls under the Virtual Firewall and is built using our integration framework using pfSense or FortiGate VM for powering the appliance.

Networking/FirewallandSecurity/Accessing.title: Accessing the VFI Control Panel
Networking/FirewallandSecurity/Accessing.description: The VFI Service on Ananta is delivered as an integration using OS images pre-loaded with pfSense or FortiGate VM. While adding Instances to the VFI-based network is possible from the Ananta Cloud Console UI, networking configurations need to be handled by accessing the VFI control panel UI.

Networking/FirewallandSecurity/AddingIns.title: Adding Instances to a VFI-based Network
Networking/FirewallandSecurity/AddingIns.description: Instances can be created and placed using the Next Generation Firewall (NGFW) Network. These Instances use special Linux OS images, which will be available as a separate OS Collection. Users can go to the usual Instance creation workflow and choose the appropriate options/controls to create an Instance in the Virtual Firewall network.

Networking/FirewallandSecurity/Applicati.title: Application Load Balancer
Networking/FirewallandSecurity/Applicati.description: The Application Load Balancer feature prevents the overloading of a single server. It efficiently distributes incoming requests to multiple available backend servers. This process involves real-time mediation of client requests, where the load balancer directs the traffic to the most suitable backend servers for optimal processing.

Networking/FirewallandSecurity/Creatinga.title: Creating a Virtual Firewall
Networking/FirewallandSecurity/Creatinga.description: To create a Virtual Firewall, follow these steps:

Networking/FirewallandSecurity/Deletinga.title: Deleting a VFI Network
Networking/FirewallandSecurity/Deletinga.description: Subscribers can unsubscribe from the Virtual Firewall service by deleting the VFI. Before deleting a VFI, any Instance(s) added to the network need to be deleted.

Networking/FirewallandSecurity/ViewingVF.title: Viewing VFI Network Details
Networking/FirewallandSecurity/ViewingVF.description: As the VFI and the VFI-based network are created, Cloud Console users can view the enabled Virtual Firewall in the Networking > Virtual Firewalls section. Clicking on this section, the following details will be shown:

Networking/IPv4-basedNetworking/AboutIPv.title: About IPv4-based Networking
Networking/IPv4-basedNetworking/AboutIPv.description: IPv4 Addresses form the backbone of networking operations on Ananta. Internally, IPv4 Addresses is treated as a monetisable Service on Ananta. However, the use of IPv4 Addresses is implicit as part of other essential Services.

Networking/IPv4-basedNetworking/Managing.title: Managing VPN Gateways and Site-to-Site VPN

Networking/IPv4-basedNetworking/UsingRem.title: Using Remote Access VPN with VPC
Networking/IPv4-basedNetworking/UsingRem.description: To use a VPN client to connect to your VPC, follow these steps:

Networking/L2 Networks/AboutL2Networks.title: About L2 Networks
Networking/L2 Networks/AboutL2Networks.description: L2 networks, often called data link layer networks, play a crucial role in computer networking by establishing direct connections between devices in a local network and ensuring data packets are delivered accurately to their intended destination.

Networking/L2 Networks/AddNewL2Networks.title: Adding New L2 Network
Networking/L2 Networks/AddNewL2Networks.description: To add a new L2 network, click on a New Network button. The following screen appears:

Networking/LoadBalancerInstances/AboutLo.title: About Load Balancer Instances
Networking/LoadBalancerInstances/AboutLo.description: The Ananta Load Balancer Instances (LBI) Service allows Cloud Console users to create load balancer appliances, which are Linux Instances pre-loaded with a Citrix NetScaler VPX image. LBI can be used with VPC networks to achieve various traffic management features that are not possible using the standard VPC-based load balancing.

Networking/LoadBalancerInstances/Activat.title: Activating the NetScaler VPX Control Panel
Networking/LoadBalancerInstances/Activat.description: The steps mentioned below can be used for activating the NetScaler VPX control panel and accessing it after activation.

Networking/LoadBalancerInstances/Creatin.title: Creating a Load Balancer Instance
Networking/LoadBalancerInstances/Creatin.description: Load Balancer Instances can be created inside a VPC by following these simple steps:

Networking/NATGateways/AboutNATGateways.title: About NAT Gateways
Networking/NATGateways/AboutNATGateways.description: A Network Address Translation (NAT) gateway is a networking component in cloud and on-premises environments that enables resources within a private subnet (such as virtual machines or containers) to connect to the internet or other external networks, without exposing these resources directly to incoming connections from those external networks. This helps in enhancing security in cloud infrastructure by keeping resources in private subnets isolated from direct public access.

Networking/NATGateways/ManagingNATGatewa.title: Managing NAT Gateways
Networking/NATGateways/ManagingNATGatewa.description: To manage a NAT gateway, follow these steps:

Networking/VirtualPrivateClouds/AboutVir.title: About Virtual Private Clouds
Networking/VirtualPrivateClouds/AboutVir.description: A Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) is a software-defined networking capability on Ananta that allows cloud users to simulate a traditional private cloud environment on a public cloud. Using VPC, users can control a host of features and configurations including subnet/tier management, access control using ACL, NAT-ing using IPv4, managing site-to-site or remote VPN connections, or in some advanced cases, terminating their MPLS connections directly on the VPC.

Networking/VirtualPrivateClouds/Creating.title: Creating VPC Subnets/Tiers
Networking/VirtualPrivateClouds/Creating.description: VPCs follow the convention of 3-tiered architectures, with web, app, and DB tiers forming the norm. You can, however, configure these tiers to suit your application architecture or just follow the common convention.

Networking/VirtualPrivateClouds/Differen.title: Difference Between L2 Networks and VPC

Networking/VirtualPrivateClouds/IPv4Addr.title: IPv4 Addresses and VPC
Networking/VirtualPrivateClouds/IPv4Addr.description: IPv4 Addresses are an integral part of using VPC networking, and need to be used to access various components of the VPC. By default, a public IPv4 Address is assigned to the VR which can communicate through the internet to transmit traffic to/from the VR. This IPv4 can also be used for configuring remote access (L2TP) and site-to-site (IPSec) VPN connections.

Networking/VirtualPrivateClouds/Managing.title: Managing Access Control on VPC Subnets
Networking/VirtualPrivateClouds/Managing.description: Access control policies can be created using Access Control Lists (ACL) and configuring rules within these ACL (called ACL Rules). An ACL can then be applied to any tier within the VPC. These policies govern what traffic is allowed or restricted into and from your VPC network tiers.

Networking/VirtualPrivateClouds/VPCManag.title: VPC Management and Basic Operations
Networking/VirtualPrivateClouds/VPCManag.description: VPC management offers the following operations. These are basic VPC management actions and don't have any impact on the actual network configurations.

Networking/VirtualPrivateClouds/Workingw.title: Working with VPN Connections in a VPC
Networking/VirtualPrivateClouds/Workingw.description: The VPC feature on Ananta provides advanced networking capabilities for use with VPN gateways and connections over an IPSec tunnel. Ananta gives the ability to create virtual private networks (VPN) to access virtual machines inside a VPC.

ResettingPassword.title: Resetting Password
ResettingPassword.description: Forgotten passwords can be reset by clicking on I have forgotten my password on Access Central.

SigningUp.title: Signing Up
SigningUp.description: Ananta offers an easy way of signing up for a subscriber cloud account. Clicking on the Sign Up link on Ananta Cloud will navigate to the signup form. Users can simply fill up this form and follow the next steps to get access to their account.

Storage/BlockVolumes/AboutBlockVolumes.title: About Block Volumes
Storage/BlockVolumes/AboutBlockVolumes.description: Ananta Block Volumes are the underlying software-defined volume management layer that powers various storage-related or storage-driven cloud Services. Block Volumes can be used with Linux and Windows Instances as root and data disks; with Kubernetes as persistent storage; or simply as a storehouse for Snapshots and Images.

Storage/BlockVolumes/UsingBlockVolumeswi.title: Using Block Volumes with Kubernetes
Storage/BlockVolumes/UsingBlockVolumeswi.description: Ananta Kubernetes (AK8s) also uses the underlying Block Volumes service to create persistent volumes for the Kubernetes clusters being created. Ananta Cloud Console users can specify the root disk size for each cluster node (control and worker) and the Block Volume service will create the nodes in the cluster with a root disk of the specified size.

Storage/BlockVolumes/WorkingwithInstance.title: Working with Instance Volumes
Storage/BlockVolumes/WorkingwithInstance.description: Each Instance on Ananta (Linux or Windows) includes a 'root' disk or volume. This is the primary disk partition on the Instance, and by default, all operating systems, data, components and files reside on the root disk.

Storage/BlockVolumes/WorkingwithVolumeSn.title: Working with Volume Snapshots
Storage/BlockVolumes/WorkingwithVolumeSn.description: The Ananta Block Volumes Service allows for extensively working with volume snapshots. Snapshots are point-in-time 'images' of the contents of a volume and can be used as a restoration point for the parent volume. The following sections outline all available snapshot functions and capabilities on Ananta Cloud Console.

Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/AboutApiculu.title: About Ananta Object Storage
Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/AboutApiculu.description: Ananta Object Storage (AS3) provides scalable and flexible storage for unstructured data in the form of objects. In object storage, data is stored as discrete objects containing data, metadata, and a unique identifier. Ananta delivers the service as an integration with Cloudian HyperStore.

Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/FeatureLista.title: Feature List and Integration Details
Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/FeatureLista.description: AS3 is delivered as an integration with Cloudian HyperStore. The integration functions using API-driven checkpoints.

Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/FrequentlyAs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/FrequentlyAs.description: What is object storage in the context of Ananta?

ToolsandUtilities/AboutToolsandUtilities.title: About Tools and Utilities
ToolsandUtilities/AboutToolsandUtilities.description: The Tools and Utilities sections on Ananta Cloud Console keep a record of cloud management utilities and assets that might be needed to use specific sections of the Ananta-powered cloud.

ToolsandUtilities/ManagingCustomTemplate.title: Managing Custom Templates and Images
ToolsandUtilities/ManagingCustomTemplate.description: You can create custom OS templates from a Volume Snapshot.  To locate these custom templates, navigate to Tools and Utilities > My Images with the following details:

ToolsandUtilities/ManagingInstanceandVol.title: Managing Instance and Volume Snapshots
ToolsandUtilities/ManagingInstanceandVol.description: Snapshots use the Ananta Block Volumes Service and occupy billable storage space.

ToolsandUtilities/ManagingSSHKeysandKeyP.title: Managing SSH Keys and Key Pairs for Linux Instances
ToolsandUtilities/ManagingSSHKeysandKeyP.description: Ananta Cloud Console allows you to create secure shell connections to Instances in your account by using SSH keys, in addition to using Instance passwords. Passwords, due to their very nature, can easily be compromised. On the other hand, SSH keys are encrypted signatures that function only when there’s a match between their public and private components.
